title: quercus aquatica var. nana
intro: Quercus aquatica var. nana, also known as dwarf water oak, is a small shrub or tree native to the southeastern United States, characterized by its compact size and rounded crown. The medicinal properties of Quercus aquatica var. nana lie in its tannins, which have been traditionally used to treat various health issues. The health benefits of this plant include its anti-inflammatory and astringent properties, which can help reduce fever, alleviate diarrhea, and soothe digestive issues. Quercus aquatica var. nana can be prepared as an herbal tea, infused in vinegar, or used in a tincture to harness its medicinal properties.
